context.meal: buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, and ranch dressing

episode_text:
day=28; meal=buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, and ranch dressing; setting=Social with person on Left; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize left occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=outside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=medium; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is fruit or a cold dessert AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves a quiet personal setting, watching TV in front, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop in front, or social with a person on the left, THEN no microwave is common, but 1 min also occurs for mixed dessert meals in TV-right and TV-left contexts BECAUSE the user usually keeps intended-cold foods unheated, yet has repeatedly chosen light warming for bananas/brownies/chocolate-sauce-type desserts. IF food is a warm savory entree with sauce already incorporated AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves working on a laptop on the left, social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, watching TV on the right, or a quiet personal setting, THEN 2 min is the main preference overall, and the new social-left buffalo-chicken meal strengthens 2 min as the stable social-left savory baseline, while 1 min can still occur as a lighter-reheat exception in some quiet-personal or left-work savory meals BECAUSE repeated savory selections support 2 min as the baseline reheating choice. IF food is a warm savory meal with separate components suitable for reheating AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves working on a laptop on the right, watching TV in front, or a quiet personal setting, THEN 3 min can occur, but 1 min also occurs for a dippable savory meal while working on a laptop in front BECAUSE the user has shown stronger reheating for some warm savory meals in those contexts, though separate dips/condiments can shift some meals toward lighter reheating. IF food is a warm savory meal with a separate dip or condiment AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves watching TV in front, working on a laptop in front, social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, or a quiet personal setting, THEN 2 min is now the better-supported social baseline, while 1 min and 3 min also occur in other contexts BECAUSE dippable savory meals do not force a single reheating level, but the new social-left ranch meal shows the user can still prefer the standard moderate reheating choice in a social setting. No broader affect-dependent variation observed yet.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE this has been selected repeatedly to avoid blocking a social partner on the right. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN minimize left occlusion is now the better-supported pattern overall, though do not consider occlusion has also occurred BECAUSE partner-aware left-side visibility has been selected multiple times and is reinforced by the new social-left meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly preserved visibility toward a front-facing attentional target. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user consistently preferred to preserve visibility toward a right-side attentional target in those meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated right-TV dessert evidence shows that right-side attentional contexts do not always trigger occlusion minimization for lighter cold foods. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left, working on a laptop on the left, or a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated selections show the user often accepts no occlusion constraint when no salient front/right social or task target is present. Overall, social partner location and front-facing or right-side media/work are the strongest drivers of occlusion minimization.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN fast is common but medium can also occur BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals favored quicker interaction, while later front-TV evidence showed the user does not always want maximum speed in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN fast BECAUSE repeated right-TV dessert evidence supports a quicker pace for light media-focused meals. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN fast BECAUSE front-laptop savory evidence shows the user prioritizes quick interaction while multitasking in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN fast BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may prioritize quick interaction in that media-focused context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN fast BECAUSE the user preferred a quicker pace while multitasking. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated social-right savory meals support a moderate pace rather than a faster one. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN medium is now the better-supported pattern overall BECAUSE the new social-left savory meal aligns with the user's broader tendency to keep a moderate pace in social dining rather than maximizing speed. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with or without a separate dip/condiment, THEN medium is the better-supported baseline overall, though fast can occur for some private dippable meals BECAUSE private savory evidence continues to favor a moderate pace more often than a fast one. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN medium is better supported overall BECAUSE repeated right-TV savory meals favor a moderate pace rather than a fast one. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left with other observed foods, social with a person on the right with other observed foods, social with a person on the left with other observed foods, watching TV on the left with other observed foods, working on a laptop in front with other observed foods, or a quiet personal setting with other observed foods AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN medium BECAUSE the user usually retained a moderate pace in those contexts. No broader affect-dependent variation observed yet.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user has consistently selected this option across meals, favoring bite acquisition reliability. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN no BECAUSE the user has consistently skipped explicit web confirmation of bite acquisition, indicating a stable preference to reduce interaction overhead. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the left, watching TV in front, working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN inside mouth transfer is common BECAUSE the user usually chose direct delivery when attention was occupied. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN outside mouth transfer is the better-supported pattern overall, though inside mouth transfer also occurs for a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment BECAUSE repeated social-right meals support the less intrusive user-reachable style overall, while one earlier meal showed convenience can sometimes override that baseline. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN outside mouth transfer is now better supported overall BECAUSE the new social-left savory meal adds direct support for the same less intrusive, socially comfortable style already seen in some left-social dining. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN both outside mouth transfer and inside mouth transfer have been selected, with outside mouth transfer still slightly better supported overall BECAUSE earlier right-TV savory meals favored the less intrusive user-reachable style, while later evidence showed the user can also choose direct delivery in that same context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE repeated right-TV dessert evidence shows that in the same environmental context the user switches to lower-effort direct delivery for lighter cold foods. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN outside mouth transfer remains the better-supported baseline overall, but inside mouth transfer can occur for warm savory meals with a separate dip/condiment BECAUSE personal-setting evidence mostly supports the less intrusive style, while multiple private dippable savory meals show that convenience can override that baseline. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE private cold-meal evidence shows the user may prefer lower-effort direct delivery for light foods. Across observed contexts, the user is physically capable of outside-mouth transfer but often prefers inside-mouth transfer when convenience or divided attention appears more salient.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, or watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN medium BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected an outside-mouth distance that balances comfort and reachability in those settings, and the new social-left meal reinforces this pattern. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal without a strong convenience cue, THEN medium is the baseline, but far can occur for a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment BECAUSE earlier private meals supported a balanced distance, while one private dippable savory meal showed the user can prefer a more self-directed reach in private. IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer, THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter does not apply when the robot delivers food directly into the mouth.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no cue was previously common but LED also occurs BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals initially showed a preference to remove readiness notifications, while later evidence shows the user may retain a lightweight visual cue rather than fully suppressing prompts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN no cue BECAUSE repeated right-TV dessert evidence supports fully unobtrusive readiness signaling in that media-focused context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN no cue BECAUSE front-laptop savory evidence shows the user suppresses readiness prompts while actively multitasking. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN no cue BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may suppress readiness prompts entirely in a media-focused context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN no cue BECAUSE the user suppressed readiness notifications while engaged in a concurrent task. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN speech BECAUSE the user selected an explicit auditory cue for fruit meals in work contexts. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN no cue BECAUSE private cold-meal evidence shows the user may prefer fully unobtrusive interaction for light meals. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN both no cue and speech + LED have occurred, with speech + LED now somewhat better supported BECAUSE one private dippable savory meal favored minimal interruption, while multiple private savory meals show the user often accepts explicit multimodal prompting in private. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right or a quiet personal setting with other observed foods AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN speech + LED is the better-supported baseline BECAUSE the user selected the most explicit multimodal readiness cue in those contexts. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN LED is the better-supported choice overall BECAUSE social-right meals support a stable preference for lightweight visual cueing in that social context. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN LED is now the better-supported choice overall, though speech + LED has also occurred BECAUSE the new social-left meal reinforces the broader social tendency toward lighter visual-only cueing rather than verbal prompting. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left with other observed foods or watching TV on the right with warm savory foods AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any other observed food, THEN LED and speech + LED have both occurred BECAUSE the user often retained some explicit cueing in those contexts, and right-TV savory meals show that full multimodal prompting can also be preferred there. Overall, social and attention-occupied contexts often reduce cue intensity relative to quiet personal settings.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button is the better-supported choice overall, though autocontinue also occurs for a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment BECAUSE repeated social-right meals support explicit control to avoid confusion with talking, while one earlier meal showed the user may instead favor lower-effort automation. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button is now the better-supported choice overall, though open mouth has also been selected BECAUSE the new social-left meal strengthens the pattern that social conversation contexts often push the user toward explicit initiation rather than mouth-based detection. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue was previously common but button also occurs BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals favored fully automatic initiation, while later front-TV evidence showed the user can shift back to explicit control. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation while multitasking. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE front-laptop savory evidence reinforces that front-facing work contexts can favor maximum automation for feeding initiation. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user shifted to a low-effort but still user-driven initiation method for fruit meals in work contexts. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user again preferred low-effort embodied initiation rather than button press or full automation. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN open mouth is the better-supported baseline overall, though autocontinue can occur for a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment BECAUSE private-setting evidence otherwise supports low-effort user-driven initiation. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE private cold-meal evidence shows that in a low-demand private context with light food, the user may prefer maximum automation over explicit initiation.

IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN button is the better-supported choice overall, though autocontinue also occurs for a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment BECAUSE social-right evidence reinforces explicit control for drink initiation in that social context, while one earlier meal showed the user can prefer lower-effort automatic initiation. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN button is now the better-supported choice overall BECAUSE the new social-left meal adds direct support for explicit drink-initiation control in social conversation settings. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN both autocontinue and button have occurred, with button still slightly better supported overall BECAUSE one private dippable savory meal favored automatic drink initiation, while another private dippable savory meal and other private savory evidence support explicit control. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE private cold-meal evidence supports lower-effort automatic drink initiation for light meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may also favor automatic drink initiation in that media-focused context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE repeated right-TV dessert evidence supports automatic drink initiation in that same media-focused light-food context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE front-laptop savory evidence shows the user prefers lower-effort drink initiation while multitasking in front. IF dining context is social with a person on the left, social with a person on the right, watching TV on the right with warm savory foods, watching TV on the right with other observed foods, watching TV on the left with other observed foods, working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop in front with other observed foods, or a quiet personal setting with other observed foods AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any other observed food, THEN button is the better-supported choice overall BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose explicit signaling for drink initiation in those contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue was previously common but button also occurs BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals had shown a preference for automatic drink initiation, while later evidence demonstrates that explicit control can still be preferred there. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected a lower-effort but still user-driven initiation method in that right-work context.

IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE private cold-meal evidence supports lower-effort automatic wiping initiation for light meals. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE front-laptop savory evidence shows the user prefers lower-effort wiping initiation while multitasking in front. IF dining context is social with a person on the left, social with a person on the right, watching TV on the right with warm savory foods, watching TV on the right with other observed foods, watching TV on the left with other observed foods, working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop in front with other observed foods, or a quiet personal setting with other observed foods AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any other observed food, THEN button is the better-supported choice overall BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose explicit signaling for mouth-wiping initiation in those contexts. detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, watching TV on the left, working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop on the right, working on a laptop in front, or a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently preferred reliable automatic detection for bite completion rather than requiring extra manual input across all observed contexts, including the new social-left savory meal. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may prefer fully automatic drink completion in that media-focused context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN autocontinue is now better supported overall, though button has also occurred BECAUSE repeated right-TV dessert evidence favors lower-effort automatic completion for light media-focused meals, while an earlier right-TV fruit meal showed the user can still choose explicit completion. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE prior right-work evidence indicates that when attention is strongly occupied in that context, reduced interaction burden can override explicit control.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may prefer fully automatic mouth-wiping completion in that media-focused context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN autocontinue is now better supported overall, though button has also occurred BECAUSE repeated right-TV dessert evidence favors lower-effort automatic completion for light media-focused meals, while an earlier right-TV fruit meal showed the user can still choose explicit completion. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN button is the better-supported choice overall BECAUSE repeated social-right savory evidence favors deliberate user signaling for mouth-wiping completion in that social context. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN button is now better supported overall BECAUSE the new social-left savory meal adds direct support for explicit mouth-wiping completion signaling in social dining. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE prior right-work evidence indicates that when attention is occupied, the user can prefer lower-effort automatic progression.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN yes is the better-supported pattern overall BECAUSE repeated social-right meals indicate a stable tendency to reduce prolonged visual obstruction in that social context, while one earlier meal showed the user may instead prioritize faster, less interrupted interaction. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN yes is now the better-supported pattern overall, though no has also occurred BECAUSE the new social-left meal strengthens the tendency to accept longer interaction in order to reduce obstruction for a left-side social partner. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN no BECAUSE the observed right-TV savory meals support prioritizing faster, less interrupted interaction there. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no was previously common but yes also occurs BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals favored not retracting, while later front-TV evidence showed the user may accept longer interaction to reduce obstruction in that same context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN no BECAUSE front-laptop savory evidence shows the user still prioritizes faster, less interrupted interaction despite a front-facing task. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN no BECAUSE the user prioritized faster, less interrupted interaction while working. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting for fruit meals in work contexts, indicating that work contexts do not uniformly suppress retraction and that lighter foods can coincide with greater willingness to accept longer interaction for less obstruction. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN no is the better-supported baseline BECAUSE both private savory and private cold-dessert meals support faster, less interrupted interaction when no social or attentional visibility concern is present.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a separate dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like, THEN less is the better-supported choice overall, though do not dip also occurs BECAUSE social-right dippable meals often support some dip while limiting messiness, but the user also repeatedly declines dipping in some social-right savory contexts. Overall, social settings and private savory meals with a clear dip support less rather than more.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN 10 sec can occur, but 1000 sec has also been selected in that environmental context BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may sometimes prefer rapid automatic progression there, while earlier left-TV evidence supported very long intervention time, so this context remains variable.
